description_long: This object is a large, multi-barreled rotary rifle resembling a Gatling or minigun, characterized by six parallel barrels arranged in a circular cluster. The main body is robust and cylindrical, with a prominent drum-shaped component likely serving as an ammunition feed or power source. A curved handle is attached to the rear, providing a grip for operation, and a secondary handle or support is visible on the top rear. The surface is uniformly dark, likely matte black, with metallic highlights suggesting a steel or alloy construction. The overall design is utilitarian and heavy-duty, suitable for high-volume fire in urban defense or assault scenarios.
description: A heavy, multi-barreled rotary rifle with a drum feed and dual handles, finished in matte black metal.
description_view_0: Front view showing the six-barrel cluster and the circular arrangement of the barrels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the barrels, the drum feed, and the rear handle assembly.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the main body, the handle, and the drum feed attachment.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the full length of the barrels and the drum feed.
